theory.canonical_statement: The Trawinistic connection generalizes the Levi-Civita connection so that covariant differentiation remains meaningful in a manifold with a degenerate metric at the zero point. It agrees with ordinary geometry away from the anchor and acquires singular behavior only where the null sector requires it.
theory.technical_interpretation: The Trawinistic connection \$\textbackslash{}nabla\$T\textbackslash{}textbackslash nabla\textbackslash{}\^{}\{\}\textbackslash{}\{\textbackslash{}textbackslash text\textbackslash{}\{T\textbackslash{}\}\textbackslash{}\}\textbackslash{}nabla\$T is the unique affine connection satisfying (i) metric compatibility away from ppp, \$\textbackslash{}nabla\$Tg=0\textbackslash{}textbackslash nabla\textbackslash{}\^{}\{\}\textbackslash{}\{\textbackslash{}textbackslash text\textbackslash{}\{T\textbackslash{}\}\textbackslash{}\} g = 0\$\textbackslash{}nabla\$Tg=0; (ii) torsion T(\$\textbackslash{}nabla\$T)=0T(\textbackslash{}textbackslash nabla\textbackslash{}\^{}\{\}\textbackslash{}\{\textbackslash{}textbackslash text\textbackslash{}\{T\textbackslash{}\}\textbackslash{}\})=0T(\$\textbackslash{}nabla\$T)=0 outside ppp; (iii) \$\textbackslash{}nabla\$T\textbackslash{}textbackslash nabla\textbackslash{}\^{}\{\}\textbackslash{}\{\textbackslash{}textbackslash text\textbackslash{}\{T\textbackslash{}\}\textbackslash{}\}\textbackslash{}nabla\$T reduces to the Levi-Civita connection when det⁡g\$\textbackslash{}neq\$0\textbackslash{}textbackslash det g\textbackslash{}textbackslash neq 0detg=0; and (iv) \$\textbackslash{}nabla\$T\textbackslash{}textbackslash nabla\textbackslash{}\^{}\{\}\textbackslash{}\{\textbackslash{}textbackslash text\textbackslash{}\{T\textbackslash{}\}\textbackslash{}\}\textbackslash{}nabla\$T annihilates null vectors at ppp.\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}

canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[
\nabla^{\mathrm{T}} g = 0
\qquad \text{away from } p
\]

\qquad \text{away from } p

\[
\nabla^{\mathrm{T}}\big\vert_{\det g \neq 0}
=
\nabla^{\mathrm{LC}},
\qquad
\nabla^{\mathrm{T}} n = 0
\ \text{for}\ n \in \operatorname{Null}_{p}(M)
\]

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This ensures the connection preserves the metric structure wherever the geometry is regular.
This aligns the operator with standard affine geometry in the ordinary part of the manifold.
This closes the progression by specifying exactly how the connection behaves both away from and at the singular anchor.

external_sources.title: Trawinistic Connection
external_sources.canonical_claim: The Trawinistic connection generalizes the Levi-Civita connection so that covariant differentiation remains meaningful in a manifold with a degenerate metric at the zero point. It agrees with ordinary geometry away from the anchor and acquires singular behavior only where the null sector requires it.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Levi‑Civita connection) supports the theorem analogue (metric‑compatible connection) by showing that This reference states that on a Riemannian manifold there is a unique torsion‑free connection compatible with the metric, known as the Levi‑Civita connection.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: This reference states that on a Riemannian manifold there is a unique torsion‑free connection compatible with the metric, known as the Levi‑Civita connection. It is the canonical connection used to differentiate tensors and preserve the metric. This provides the starting point for generalising the connection to a degenerate metric.

promotion_ledger.note: Append-only proof-support promotion; final physical certificate still requires review of cited source fit.
